Tomato deficiencies: Nitrogen (yellowing), Phosphorus (purplish leaves), Potassium (leaf browning), Magnesium (V-shape yellowing), Calcium (blossom-end rot).
Ideal soil pH: Tomatoes 6.0-6.8, Peas 6.0-7.0. Acceptable: Tomatoes 5.5-7.0, Peas 5.5-7.5. pH affects nutrient availability.

User started model building again ~6 weeks ago. First airbrush attempt on last project, learning curve. Wants to improve airbrushing skills.
Airbrushing tips: dual-action airbrush, 10-15 PSI, thin paints 1:1-2 ratio, clean regularly. Techniques: 90° angle 6-8 inches, smooth motions, layering, edge control.
Books: The Airbrush Bible (Tim Gore), Airbrushing for Scale Modelers (Brett Green), Art of Airbrushing (David Doyle), Scale Model Airbrushing (Tony Green).
Online forums: Hyperscale, Scalemates, Model Mayhem, Reddit r/ModelBuilding. YouTube: Model Builder's Workshop, Scale Model TV.
Dual-action better for model building: finer control, variable flow, better detail work. Single-action only for general-purpose use.
Airbrush brands: Badger (Renegade Krome, Patriot), Paasche (H, TG), Harder & Steenbeck (Ultra, Evolution), Iwata (Eclipse).
Model building compressor specs: 10-30 PSI, 0.5-2 CFM flow. Should be quiet, compact, have moisture control, low power consumption.
Compressors: Badger (20 PSI, 0.5 CFM, quiet), Paasche (25 PSI, 1.2 CFM), Iwata (25 PSI, 1.5 CFM). All have moisture control.
Pancake compressors pros: compact, quiet, energy-efficient, low-cost. Cons: limited air storage, lower pressure (10-15 PSI), short duty cycle, light-duty only.
Oil-free compressors: cleaner air, less maintenance, quieter, lighter, increased safety, consistent air supply for better atomization and finish.
